summary: Back at a World Cup after 28 years, chasing a first-ever run past the group stage. Steve Clarke shifts between a back four and a back five depending on the opponent, with a heavy reliance on crosses and attacking set pieces — and Scott McTominay's late runs into the box the standout threat.
style: Flexible — a back four (4-2-3-1 / 4-3-3 in qualifying) or a back five (as vs Ivory Coast), chosen by opposition. Crosses into the box and dead balls are the core attacking routes; McTominay roams to support progression in wide areas, or they go direct and fight for second balls. Likely a mid-block against Brazil and Morocco, hunting transitions and set pieces.

counterReliance.note: Mid-block vs strong sides; capitalise on transitions and set pieces

strengths: Set-piece reliant — 38% of qualifying goals from dead balls, 2nd-highest in Europe behind Czechia
Crossing volume into the box, served by wing-backs and an overlapping LCB (Tierney)
Tactical flexibility between a four and a five
weaknesses: Lowest possession share (46%) of any qualified European side — reliant on transitions and dead balls
Limited sustained attacking quality against the better teams

keyPlayers.name: Scott McTominay
keyPlayers.note: Late runs into the box (SkillCorner cross-receiver 93/99); scored a spectacular overhead kick in the play-off win over Denmark

keyPlayers.name: Ben Doak
keyPlayers.note: Pace to burn, fearless two-footed dribbler with an improving final ball — potential breakout star

keyPlayers.name: John McGinn
keyPlayers.note: Big-moment goalscorer who arrives in the box

tactics: Defend in a mid-block, force play wide, and protect the box, then threaten through crosses, second balls and set pieces — the dead-ball threat is the key weapon. Eight third-placed teams reach the Round of 32, so even a strong group third could finally break the knockout duck.
notes: In eight previous World Cups Scotland have never reached the knockout stage; a first knockout match in any major tournament is on the table with the expanded 2026 format.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group C (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
